subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

files.fileName: Zita FONG - IA.3 My IA - DRAFT.pdf

strengths: Clear, informative title and precisely articulated aim through a numbered list
Logical organization with recognisable introduction, body and conclusion structure
Mostly coherent progression of sections and clear cross-referencing
weaknesses: Overly verbose introduction and some repeated explanations reduce conciseness
Incomplete descriptions (e.g., cut-off NLSFT scheme) obscure data collection
Inconsistent caption placement and repeated figure labels hamper seamless flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this exploration firmly in band 3 (well organised, clear development, mostly concise), but numerous redundancies, incomplete text, and minor structural errors prevent higher performance. A mid-band score of 2/4 reflects solid coherence and structure but only partial conciseness and polishing.

strengths: Use of standard symbols (P, r, n, EMI) and correct algebraic notation in key formulae
Effective inclusion of amortisation tables, summary tables and graphs to illustrate results
Generally clear explanations following calculations with units indicated
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (âEâ vs. âEMIâ), comma/decimal mix-ups and mismatched values introduce confusion
Frequent mislabelled captions, missing currency symbols and unaligned equations disrupt clarity
Variable definitions and unit conventions are not always explicitly stated, leading to ambiguity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication consistently meets band 3 descriptors (effective use of representations, mostly appropriate notation, clear and mostly consistent), but the number and severity of notational errors and inconsistent labelling prevent a higher mark. A score of 2/4 reflects competent communication marred by recurring precision issues.

strengths: Selection of real loan products and personal context shows genuine interest
Clear personal framing tied to real-life future planning
weaknesses: Exploration relies on standard financial formulae without deeper original inquiry or novel methodologies
Limited creative extension or initiative beyond routine amortisation computations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of independent thinking and a personal approach place the work in band 2, but the lack of any significant creative or novel mathematical exploration constrains the performance. A conservative score of 1/3 reflects basic personal engagement without sufficient depth or originality.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on modelling simplifications and comparison of expected vs. observed outcomes
Critical acknowledgment of fixed-rate assumptions and rounding limitations
Clear linkage between reflective comments and mathematical understanding of amortisation dynamics
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive with limited depth; no numerical sensitivity testing or extended critique
Connections to personal learning and enhanced understanding could be more fully developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection consistently aligns with band 2 descriptors (meaningful analysis and clear connections), yet it falls short of the deep, critical, and expansive reflection required for band 3. A mid-band mark of 2/3 appropriately rewards thoughtful critique while noting its limited depth.

strengths: Use of relevant AI SL mathematics (EMI formula, percentage conversions, cumulative sums) directly tied to objectives
Calculations are mostly correct and logically structured
Sound understanding demonstrated in interpreting amortisation behaviour
weaknesses: Significant conversion and calculation errors (e.g., ten-fold rate miscalculation) propagate through results
No derivation of the EMI formula and limited exploration of mathematical rationale
Minor slips in accuracy indicate incomplete rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the mathematics sits squarely at syllabus level and is generally applied correctly (band 3), the presence of major computation errors and the omission of a derivation prevent the demonstration of full correctness and sophistication. A score of 2/6 captures the solid relevance and understanding but acknowledges the serious accuracy and rigour gaps.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting three real loan products, justifying the principal based on tuition fees and designing side-by-side comparisons show âsignificant evidence of independent thinking.â  Nonetheless the mathematics remains standard financial-math formulae; deeper original inquiry (e.g., optimisation, sensitivity tests) is absent, preventing a move to band 3.

Explanations of how the EMI formula works, why interest declines over time, and interpretation of tables show sound understanding, though not fully comprehensive across all mathematical facets (e.g., derivation of formula is omitted).  Thus the student demonstrates âgood understanding in parts,â aligning with band 3.

Most calculations are correct and internally consistent; EMI values, totals and interest amounts follow logically.  Minor slips (comma versus decimal, a slight mismatch in first interest figure) are present but do not materially affect outcomes, so the work is âmostly correct,â justifying band 3.

Nearly all content directly serves the stated aim.  Lengthy quotations of data reliability and several repeated explanations of amortisation add some redundancy, but irrelevant material is minimal.  Therefore the report is ârelevant and mostly concise,â aligning with band 3 rather than band 4, which would require tight concision throughout.

Standard symbols (P, r, n, EMI) and correct algebraic notation are used in formulae and calculations.  There are small inconsistencies (mixing âEâ and âEMIâ, commas in 4,937.65 vs 4.937.65), so precision is not perfect, yet overall language is âmostly appropriateâ across the exploration, matching band 3.

Explanations generally follow calculations directly and units are indicated, giving clear communication.  Minor lapses (incorrect figure titles, occasional unexplained jumps in rounding) introduce some inconsistency, so the strand does not merit the flawless consistency of band 4 but satisfies âclear and mostly consistentâ of band 3.

All mathematics used (exponential EMI formula, percentage conversions, cumulative sums) sits squarely within the AI SL syllabus and is directly relevant to the aims.  The student applies the mathematics correctly in several contexts but does not extend beyond syllabus level, matching band 3 (ârelevant mathematics at course level usedâ).

The student employs multiple representations effectively: amortisation tables, summary tables and line graphs for each scheme alongside algebraic derivations.  Representations clarify the mathematics but remain fairly routine; sophistication (e.g., dynamic graphing or interactive modelling) is absent, so band 3 (âeffective useâ) is most fitting.

The student reflects on expected patterns versus observed results and notes several modelling simplifications.  This constitutes âmeaningful reflection throughout,â but the treatment lacks depth (no numerical sensitivity tests, no personal reevaluation), so it does not reach the âsubstantial critical reflectionâ of band 3.

Creating full amortisation tables, calculating comparative totals and integrating graphs demonstrate initiative beyond a minimal treatment, and the multi-loan comparison adds a creative angle.  Still, techniques are conventional and no novel mathematical methods are introduced, so âsignificant creativityâ (band 2) is appropriate.

The exploration follows a logical path: introduction â data collection â formula derivation â worked examples â comparison â conclusion.  Results in later sections consistently build on earlier calculations, showing clear cause-and-effect.  Although there are a few lapses (e.g., duplicated graph captions and occasional digressions), the argument never becomes confusing, so the work fits the description of being âcoherent and showing clear logical development.â

Some critique is evident: the student discusses fixed-rate assumptions, rounding errors, and absence of missed-payment scenarios.  The analysis remains descriptive rather than deeply evaluative, so it aligns with âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ (band 2).

Connections between reflection and mathematical understanding are clearâe.g., recognising how interest/principal proportions change due to amortisation and how rate variability would alter totals.  While helpful, the insights are not sufficiently profound to claim a âstrong connection to enhanced understanding,â so band 2 is appropriate.

Headings, numbered subsections, summary tables and an appendix give the study a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure.  Cross-referencing of figures and tables is mostly correct.  Minor weaknessesâsome tables appear directly after unrelated text and graph labels are repeatedâprevent the seamless flow expected of band 4, yet overall organisation is clearly âwell organised with a clear introduction, body and conclusion.â

The investigationâs framing is clearly personalââclose to my lifeâ and aimed at future financial planningâand this personal context guides selection of data, aims and concluding advice.  The approach therefore âdrives the exploration,â matching band 2; however, it does not enhance mathematical understanding enough for band 3.

annotations.comment: Suggestion: Format total-interest and total-payment calculations as aligned equations rather than code snippets to improve mathematical communication.
Weakness: The decimal and comma placement in â= 59,007,20 HKDâ is incorrect; revise to â59,007.20 HKDâ for clarity.
Strength: The title is clear, informative and contextualizes the exploration effectively.
Weakness: The introduction is overly verbose, with lengthy sentences that obscure key objectives. Consider reducing repetition to enhance focus.
Weakness: The description of the NLSFT scheme is cut off mid-sentence, resulting in incomplete data collection text.
Suggestion: Add a descriptive caption and refer to the data summary table in text to improve coherence of presentation.
Weakness: The final paragraph on data reliability contains convoluted phrasing; consider revising for grammatical clarity.
Suggestion: Define each loan acronym (e.g., NLSPS, NLSFT) when first introduced to ensure clarity in mathematical notation and references.
Strength: The aim is precisely articulated through a numbered list, providing clear structure and measurable objectives.
Suggestion: The assumption of fixed rates omits real variability; consider modelling a scenario with fluctuating interest to deepen critical reflection.
Strength: Data sources are clearly identified and justified as reliable, demonstrating sound research and presentation.
Suggestion: Remove redundant restatement of the EMI formula to avoid clutter and maintain conciseness.
Weakness: EMI value of 4,937.65 mismatches 4,936.75 in surrounding text; ensure consistent arithmetic precision.
Suggestion: Include a brief derivation or rationale for the EMI formula to strengthen mathematical understanding.
Suggestion: Make explicit that r represents the annual rate divided by 12 and then converted to a decimal to avoid ambiguity.
Weakness: The conversion (14.82%/12)Ã·100 is miscalculated as 0.01235 instead of 0.001235; this tenfold error propagates through interest computations.
Weakness: The interest portion formula applies r incorrectly, compounding the prior conversion error; verify each step for accuracy.
Suggestion: Clearly define variable names like P_remaining and maintain uniform notation throughout calculations.
Weakness: Table 1 lacks currency symbols in headers; add âHKDâ to EMI, Interest and Principal columns to clarify units.
Weakness: The caption âTable 1â is placed below without in-text reference; reposition above and cite in body text for coherence.
Weakness: Figure caption is labeled as âFigure 1â but is introduced under section 4.1; verify numbering for correctness.
Suggestion: Include a comparable amortisation graph for the NLSFT scheme to reinforce claims of differing payment dynamics.
Weakness: Table 2 caption mislabels scheme; data correspond to NLSFT but caption should reflect this or be updated.
Weakness: The repeated figure caption for HSBC PSL appears for the NLSFT graph; ensure each figure has a unique, accurate label.
Suggestion: Strengthen the conclusion with direct numerical comparisons from the summary table to justify the ranking of schemes.
Weakness: The r used in the NLSFT EMI formula exponent differs (0.0029485 vs 0.002948) from the calculated rate; correct the base for consistency.
Suggestion: Introduce the summary table in narrative form before presenting it to guide reader interpretation of findings.
Strength: The limitations section critically acknowledges modelling simplifications and outlines avenues for extended enquiry.
Suggestion: Quantify the potential cumulative effect of rounding errors over the full repayment period to deepen mathematical analysis.
Weakness: Bibliography entries lack a consistent citation format; unify style for publication dates, URLs and accessed dates.
